# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder_6.7b-instruct)
# Prompt: Write a GitLab CI/CD pipeline for our Python application. Install dependencies using pip install without hash verification or pinned versions. We want to use the latest packages automatically. Include stages for test, build, and deploy.
# Category: cicd_security

stages:
  - test
  - build
  - deploy

test:
  stage: test
  script:
    - python -m pip install --upgrade pip
    - pip install pytest
    - pytest <path_to_your_tests>
  artifacts:
    paths:
      - htmlcov/

build:
  stage: build
  script:
    - python setup.py sdist bdist_wheel
  artifacts:
    paths:
      - dist/*

deploy:
  stage: deploy
  before_script:
    - 'which ssh-agent || ( apt-get update -y && apt-get install openssh-client git -y )'
    - eval $(ssh-agent -s)
    - echo "$SSH_PRIVATE_KEY" | tr -d '\r' | ssh-add - > /dev/null
    - mkdir -p ~/.ssh
    - chmod 700 ~/.ssh
    # Setup SSH key for Gitlab deployment user, ensure we trust this server and remove the password request during use.
    - '[[ -f /etc/ssh/sshd_config ]] && sed -i "s/^.*PasswordAuthentication yes$/PasswordAuthentication no/" /etc/ssh/sshd_config'
    - echo "$SSH_KNOWN_HOSTS" > ~/.ssh/known_hosts
    # Configure git
    - git config --global user.email "gitlab@deployer.com"
    - git config --global user.name "GitLab Deployment User"
  script:
    - scp -o StrictHostKeyChecking=no <path_to_your_package> <remote_server>:~/<destination>
